implementation.normal_path: For a staged chunked entry whose file chunks all carry expected size and CRC32 metadata, combine chunk CRC32 values by length and compare the result with minizip-ng's completed-entry CRC32.
implementation.failure_path: On a combined CRC mismatch, reread expected file chunks only on the failure path so diagnostics retain ZIP entry, chunk index and file context.
implementation.fallback: Entries without complete staged CRC metadata retain per-file-chunk validation; raw compressed copy and stored-bootstrap behavior are unchanged.

compatibility.raw_copy_payload_accounting: Every warm-up and measured matrix output passed planned raw compressed-payload equality and logical byte accounting.
compatibility.stale_chunk_failure: Focused tests preserved failure-before-state-change, chunk-specific diagnostics, output protection and retry after staged file mutation.

interpretation.supported_claim: On this machine and the recorded one-million-cell level-6 targeted-upsert workload, removing the normal-path duplicate staged-file CRC scan reduced the isolated target-entry residual median from 131892 to 603 microseconds while keeping process peak working set near 8.34 MB.
interpretation.overall_timing: The observed target-entry median also changed from 2330860 to 1669776 microseconds, but minizip write and system scheduling varied; this is not generalized as an overall throughput claim.
interpretation.next_hotspot: DEFLATE writer time and worksheet parser/action traffic dominate the remaining profiles. The next implementation lane is inline-string wrapper residual profiling; a compression backend change still requires separate dependency, output-size and compatibility evidence.

scope: Single Windows/MSVC machine, one-million-cell 10-column numeric and formula distributions, 1000 targeted upserts, source/output DEFLATE level 6. No cross-machine, arbitrary-workbook, Office, parallel-compression or OpenXLSX superiority claim.
